experiment: query-adaptive funnel width on Cohere cell N=100k, 1000 queries, rotate x2. Adaptive C_q = #candidates within hamming[k-1]+margin. Compared to fixed C; certificate = Hamming gap at C=100 vs per-query recall.
note: Adaptive-margin C cuts mean rerank ~34% at matched high recall: margin=32 -> 0.9939 @ mean_C=328 vs fixed C=500 -> 0.9951. Wins in the high-recall operating band (~0.97-0.995); OVERSHOOTS at the extreme tail (margin=64: 0.9997 @ mean_C=1753 > fixed C=1000 @ 0.9985) because a few hard queries blow up C_q -> the simple margin rule needs a cap / relative margin. Payoff is marginal in-RAM (scan-dominated) but DIRECT where rerank is the cost: disk-resident regime (045, ~0.4ms/SSD read) -> 328 vs 500 reads = ~34% latency cut. Certificate VALIDATED: per-query Hamming gap predicts recall (gap 7-14 -> 0.776, gap>=15 -> 0.941), a compute-without-truth miss-risk signal enabling per-query SLA.
